field.label: Name
field.help: Identifies this reflector in the log and in the generated configuration. Letters, digits, dashes and underscores.

field.label: Source interface
field.help: The interface to listen on. This is the side the device you are looking for is not on: an mDNS query from a phone on the LAN is picked up here.

field.label: Target interface
field.help: The interface to re-emit on, with the addressing rewritten. This is the side the device you are looking for is on.

field.label: SSDP
field.help: UPnP discovery: DLNA servers, Sonos, smart TVs.

field.label: DIAL proxy
field.help: Casting to a TV ("Discovery And Launch"). Needs SSDP, and is IPv4-only, so it cannot be used on an IPv6-only reflector.

field.label: WS-Discovery
field.help: ONVIF cameras and NVRs, Windows network devices and printers.

field.label: Wake-on-LAN
field.help: Forward magic packets, so a machine on the other interface can be woken.

field.label: Wake-on-LAN ports
field.help: UDP ports magic packets arrive on. Leave empty for the daemon's defaults, 7 and 9.

field.label: Devices
field.help: MAC addresses to scope this reflector to. Leave empty to reflect for the whole network.

field.label: Address family
field.help: Which IP versions to reflect. Default requires IPv4 and adds IPv6 when the interfaces have it, so it will not start on an IPv6-only interface. Dual requires both. A required family that is missing fails startup; a best-effort one is simply skipped, and starts reflecting as soon as the address appears.
